next_step_text: Mar 27, 2026: talk with Adtian Discussion Summary: • You confirmed the decision not to proceed with signing the solution portion at this time, driven by the CIO’s broader SaaS and internal build strategy. • You reiterated that this is not a reflection on Zycus, and that you still see strong value in our domain expertise. • You outlined a desire to pivot toward a design-focused engagement to map cu

our_open_deliverables.text: Send the data-request email and firm-up attendee/governance list for Lynn to circulate before kickoff
Deliver the refined design-engagement kickoff deck (v1 shown June 12) for the July 7 session

buyer_dependent_items.text: Return SOW and MSA redline feedback to unblock the design-engagement signature
Provide existing policies, process maps, and the workshop attendee list
Name the Capitec business sponsor who will open the kickoff

competitive_position.summary: This is a build-vs-buy contest, not a vendor bake-off. The single strongest current threat is Capitec's internal-build strategy, sponsored by a newly influential CIO who paused the 218k solution deal in March 2026 on a broader SaaS/internal-build direction and remains unengaged with Zycus. No external SaaS rival is named in any source. Letsema is Zycus's co-delivery partner, not a competitor. Zycus's live wedge is the technology-agnostic design engagement, which keeps it inside the account and creates the platform-value and TCO proof needed to re-open the solution conversation post-September.

competitors.name: Internal build (Capitec in-house development)
competitors.quote: confirmed the decision not to proceed with signing the solution portion at this time, driven by the CIO's broader SaaS and internal build strategy
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify internal-build TCO and time-to-production disadvantage during the design engagement and hand the CIO a buy-vs-build case anchored to Capitec's own baseline.

champion_strength.source: Avoma S2C Review Workshop Schedule 2026-06-12; SF task/activity contact
champion_strength.summary: Lynn Dudley-Owen is a developing champion for the design engagement, driving scheduling, stakeholder coordination, and a weekly cadence, but she is delivery-side with no budget or executive authority and no line to the CIO. Adrian Jacobs is a coach, not an advocate. No champion currently carries the buy-vs-build case to power.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The true budget authority is Capitec's CIO, who has never been on a call and who paused the solution deal on a 'broader SaaS and internal build strategy' (SF Next_Step__c 2026-03-27). For the design engagement the business sponsor is still unnamed: on the 2026-06-12 call Lynn Dudley-Owen said 'we would need a sponsor... it's gotta be the bigwigs' and named Grant Hardy (top, not attending) and 'Amelia/Emilia' (representing the sponsor) as transcript-only possibilities, none of them a confirmed Salesforce contact. Adrian Jacobs (Decision Maker) manages contracting but Lynn explicitly said 'I don't think it's Adrian' for the business sponsor. Economic buyer is therefore unmapped; naming and engaging the design-engagement sponsor at the July 7 kickoff is a ranked move.

recommended_moves: Get Adrian Jacobs to return the SOW and MSA redlines to finalize the design-engagement contract.
Ask Lynn and Johan to name the Capitec business sponsor to open the July 7 kickoff.
Hold a 30-minute pre-kickoff connect with the named business sponsor before July 7.
